description: Synthetic root/reply pairs for offline engagement-intelligence tests. NO real X data, NO real handles, NO real URLs, NO real emails. Used only by __tests__/engagementIntelligence.test.ts and scripts/engagement-intelligence/analyzeEngagementSamples.js.

pairs.rootText: The new pitch clock made baseball games much shorter.
pairs.replyText: Agreed. Games are noticeably shorter and the pace is better.

pairs.rootText: Coffee shops should add quiet zones.
pairs.replyText: Yes, that sounds fair for focused work.

pairs.rootText: The pitch clock made baseball games much more serious.
pairs.replyText: Fair point, but it also rushed pitchers into mistakes.

pairs.rootText: Remote work boosts productivity by 13%.
pairs.replyText: Where is this from? Source for the 13% claim?

pairs.rootText: The mayor said zoning reform is dead on arrival.
pairs.replyText: Quote the exact bit. Which sentence said that?

pairs.rootText: Every successful product has good onboarding.
pairs.replyText: Define successful. That word is doing a lot of work here.

pairs.rootText: Tabs are overused in product UI.
pairs.replyText: Too broad. That is true for settings pages but not all of product UI.

pairs.rootText: Late commutes are caused by bike lanes taking road space.
pairs.replyText: Correlation does not equal cause. Other variables explain late commutes.

pairs.rootText: Speed matters more than quality in startup product launches.
pairs.replyText: Wrong priorities. Quality matters more than speed at this stage.

pairs.rootText: Every team that uses async stand-ups is more productive.
pairs.replyText: That does not follow. Productivity has more variables than meeting format.

pairs.rootText: Large menus mean low food quality.
pairs.replyText: Counterexample: the neighborhood diner runs a huge menu and is consistently good.

pairs.rootText: City council meetings should enforce a strict 3-minute timer.
pairs.replyText: Speaking of which, why is parking so bad downtown?

pairs.rootText: Every app should have a boring mode.
pairs.replyText: lol gamification be like 🎉🎉🎉

pairs.rootText: Read receipts make conversations worse.
pairs.replyText: Maybe.

pairs.rootText: Brunch is mostly a scheduling problem, so brunch food does not matter.
pairs.replyText: I grant brunch is a scheduling thing, but the food still matters.

pairs.rootText: Late-season games matter more now because of the play-in.
pairs.replyText: Yes, late-season games matter, but framing this as a play-in win overstates it.

pairs.rootText: Pitch clock helped baseball overall.
pairs.replyText: Source for the overall claim? But yes the games are shorter, that I grant.

pairs.rootText: Trash talk is part of sports literacy.
pairs.replyText: Fair point that trash talk is a skill, but treating it as sports literacy excuses bad behavior.

pairs.rootText: Settings pages should ship with search.
pairs.replyText: Agreed. And also: settings categories should collapse by default.

pairs.rootText: Every onboarding screen is an apology for bad UI.
pairs.replyText: Too broad. This is true for consumer apps, not for B2B enterprise apps.

pairs.rootText: Voice notes ruin team memory.
pairs.replyText: The tone here is loaded. The framing of ruin is doing a lot of work.

pairs.rootText: Group chats are bad project management.
pairs.replyText: Mostly right, but for two-person teams group chats are fine. Narrow the claim.

pairs.rootText: Tacos beat sandwiches on every metric.
pairs.replyText: Wrong on portability for very full fillings. I grant tacos win on heat retention though.

pairs.rootText: Brunch is mostly a scheduling problem.
pairs.replyText: Oh sure, totally a scheduling problem.
